headline: Coding Education Reaches Only 30% of Secondary Schools
context: Three years after the 2023 national coding education initiative, only 30% of secondary schools offer structured programming courses.

cards.big: Only 2,400 of 8,000 secondary schools have coding in their timetable by 2026
cards.sub: Teacher shortage is the primary bottleneck with only 1,800 trained instructors

cards.big: MOE trained only 1,800 coding teachers against a target of 5,000 by 2025
cards.sub: Training pipeline covers 36% of projected demand for computer science teachers

cards.big: Industry estimates 45,000 unfilled tech positions in Malaysia as of Q1 2026
cards.sub: School-level gaps in coding education contribute to downstream talent shortage

cards.big: Vietnam achieved 78% secondary school coding coverage within 4 years of mandate
cards.sub: Centralized teacher training academies accelerated Vietnam's rollout

cards.big: Digital economy ambitions require digital education—not in 10 years but now
Should coding be made compulsory for all secondary school students by 2028
